reasoning: The statistical trend model from FRED provides a baseline 63% probability that the 2-year expected inflation rate will increase, mainly driven by a slight upward trend and expected mean reversion (the current 2.469 value is below the trailing 12-month average of ~2.51). However, recent real-time inflation proxies are coming in very low, with the latest Truflation metric (as of February 8, 2026) at just 0.68% YoY and the Cleveland Fed's own CPI Nowcast for February 2026 sitting at a mild 2.34%. Since the 2-year expected inflation model integrates incoming inflation data and swaps, this surprising disinflationary signal will likely apply downward pressure on inflation expectations between February 15 and March 17. Adjusting the 63% anchor down to account for this bearish recent data yields a final probability of 53%.
